feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Process Orchestration
feed.subtitle: SAP Process Orchestration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to Edit/Trim filename in file receiver channel in SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have an SFTP to File based scenario which is passthrough (No ESR mapping involved)

Here we are picking the statement files from SFTP server and placing in File NFS Al11 path we are getting filename as CAMT05X_11551XXX_309XXX_86530XXX_20241030003216391.xml20241030-013813-047.SNL10248D11730252152604557C

We are using ASMA parameter(SFTP Sender Channel) to maintain same file name from sender SFTP to File receiver channel.

Sender SFTP channel

Receiver File channel

We need to trim or remove the .SNL10248D11730252152604557C from the file name and place it in File NFS Al11 path.

Filename is changing dynamically so how can we achieve this??

Can someone please help here?

Thanks,

Vidya.

entry.title: SAP PI Creating Dynamic URL on REST receiver adapter through UDF
entry.content:
We have an ECC to partner synchronous interface using PI 7.5. ECC sends a request to the partner through PI and the partner sends a response back. The ECC to the partner connection uses a REST receiver adapter. The interface is working fine except under some scenarios. We are using URL parameters to dynamically generate the URL.

The partner URL that PI is calling has the format   https://partnerserver/services/v2_0/stc/{id}?agencyLocationCode={ALC}&status={STATUS}&lastModifiedDateTime={LMDT}

This works just fine when ALC, STATUS and LMDT have values; so for example the following works

https://partnerserver/services/v2_0/stc/id1234?agencyLocationCode=ALC1&status=ACTIVE&lastModifiedDateTime=12-31-2024

The problem arises when ALC, STATUS and LMDT do not have values; the URL that PI is generating looks like below

https://partnerserver/services/v2_0/stc/id1234?agencyLocationCode=&status=&lastModifiedDateTime=

The application at the partner's side does not like this; they want us to use

https://partnerserver/services/v2_0/stc/id1234

So we introduced a UDF in the map to dynamically generate the URL and set the URL on the comm channel to  

https://partnerserver/services/v2_0/stc/{id}{ALC}{STATUS}{LMDT}

ALC, STATUS and LMDT are dynamic configuration variables set in the UDF. I used the approach in https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/rest-receiver-adapter-multiple-end-points/qaq-p/12452398 to set this up

The UDF writes logs to PIMON and I can see ALC, STATUS and LMDT being properly derived. However when I run the interface, I get the following mapping error in PIMON 

MP: exception caught with cause com.sap.aii.adapter.rest.ejb.parse.LiteralValueExpectedException: Expected literal value in pattern "https://partnerserver/services/v2_0/stc/{id}{ALC}{STATUS}{LMDT}" at position 45

Any ideas on how to fix this ?

entry.title: SAP PI Most messages are going into scheduled and few are processing success
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have a schenario SOAP to SOAP.

Most of the messages are in scheduled mode and only few are processing successfully.

Even unable to get log in message log for scheduled messages.

The messages which are in scheduled mode they are not going into error and success after few hours also still they are in scheduled mode only.

around 35k messages are in schedule mode they are not going to error and not going to process and unable to see the message log and nothing is there to see after few hours from 35k few are processed and few got failed so finally 25 k messages are in scheduled and they are stuck.

Please suggest.

 

Thanks & Regards,

Dileep

entry.title: Add delay in Request message SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have Synchronous interface from 3rd party non-SAP to SAP S4 backend system. We have strange requirement, saying whenever there is a request coming from 3rd party system then that request be going to sleep (or) delayed instead of directly sending it to S4 system. Sender 3rd party system should not be receiving any response back to them instead of that nothing should be sent to them.. but at the same time they should not get any response like 500, 404. etc.., however if we go and check in SAP PO monitoring then one should be able to see the messages in monitoring page of SAP PO.. Could you please guide us to achieve this requirement using SAP PO .. thank you!

Regards,

Ramu.

entry.title: IN SAP PI/PO there is soap adapter scenario (WSDL)
entry.content: There is scenario of soap adapters and in the receiver side the target wants WSDL file how to do it?

entry.title: Error in groovy script - The entity "nbsp" was referenced, but not declared
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have used a script which was running fine but suddenly there is an error seen as mentioned below-

 
Error Details
com.sap.xi.mapping.camel.XiMappingException: The entity "nbsp" was referenced, but not declared., cause: org.xml.sax.SAXParseException; lineNumber: 7; columnNumber: 200; The entity "nbsp" was referenced, but not declared.
 
On checking the payload I can see a &nbsp character seen in the input which is causing the error.
Input Payload
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<ServiceNowIncident>
    <Incident>
        <ADONumber>40000</ADONumber>
        <State></State>
        <AssignedTo></AssignedTo>
        <AdditionalComments>SRIVASTAVA Nidhi (External) Hello Laurent, thank you for sharing the examples. We are investigating and come back to you with the solution. Best regards, Val</AdditionalComments>
    </Incident>
</ServiceNowIncident>
 
The groovy used is mentioned in the below blog, bit different but more or less the same. 
Solved: Structure Formatting for Receiver in SAP CPI throu... - SAP Community
 
Please suggest if this can be achieved easily, as there are already many conditions checked in the script and adding more should not hamper the existing one as this is already running in production.
 
Thanks,
Nidhi Srivastava

We have many messages stuck on SAP PO where receiver system is also SAP PI/PO..Also nothing shows up in message logs and Could you please guide to resolve the issue, Please see below snippets..thanks!

 

 

 

Regards

Ramu

entry.title: SAP ECC to BTP Integration Suite Proxy connection
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have the following situation where sender SAP ECC system would like to send XML proxy message to BTP Integration Suite.. whereas we also have SAP PO in our landscape.. SAP ECC ABAP developer would like to get our SAP PO ESR objects reflected on their SAP ECC system.. But, in runtime the messages should flow via BTP Integration Suite.. How to acheive this , should we ask BASIS guys to create two G type destinations on SAP ECC like mentioned below:

1.  "G" type destination(SAP_PROXY_ESR) to connect to SAP PO ESR. With the help of this destination, ESR objects should be visible in SAP ECC (SPROXY).

2. Another "G" type destination to route the messages to BTP Integration Suite.

Could you please guide me to acheive the above requirement?

Please let me know if something important is missed.

 

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.title: SPROXY End to End Testing - Shop -> PI 7.5 ->S4HANA Scenario
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a question regarding a project I’m currently working on. Let me provide some background details for better context.

Background

We have a scenario that consists of four main steps:

  1. A Webshop sends an order to the Process Integration (PI) system.
  2. The PI system receives the message via SOAP, validates its structure, and forwards it to the backend system via HTTP. A valid and working HTTP destination is already configured for this.
  3. In the backend system, using SPROXY, we handle the namespace of the ESR (Enterprise Service Repository) and can view all design objects. A service provider processes the message, forwards it to an ABAP class, which extracts the relevant data, fills a BAPI, and finally creates a sales document in the SAP backend using the payload data.
  4. This scenario has been running smoothly, and the existing functionality is working perfectly.

New Requirement

The requirements have now changed. The webshop will send two new optional fields if they are available. To address this, we made the following changes:

  • The PI structure was updated to handle these two fields (this part is already completed and working).
  • In the backend, the ABAP class logic was updated to:
    • Map these new fields if provided.
    • Pass the new data into a newly declared table. This table will handle the additional fields in parallel with the existing configuration.

With this update, the new fields will be processed as follows:

  • If the fields are present in the payload, they are mapped to the new table and passed along with the original tables to the BAPI.
  • If the fields are not provided, the process will continue as before, without impacting the existing functionality.

After implementing these changes, the scenario appears to function correctly. Messages sent from the PI Test Tool are successfully delivered to the backend.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Current Challenge

When testing through the PI Test Tool, I see that the messages are being delivered to the backend. However, I am unable to verify if the sales document is being created correctly or if the new fields are included when provided.

Request

Can you guide me on how to perform an end-to-end test to ensure the functionality works as expected? Specifically:

  • Which SAP transactions can I use to check if the sales document has been created successfully and verify the inclusion of the new fields? Which data can I use for the search process?
  • If debugging is needed, how can I:
    • Import the payload into the ABAP code for testing?
    • Check if the message sent from the PI Test Tool is properly processed in the backend?

Any help in verifying this scenario end-to-end would be greatly appreciated. Thank you in advance for your support!

entry.title: Outh2.0 option not available in REST Sender Adapter in PI/PO System
entry.content:
Our PI/POsystem version is SAP NetWeaver 7.50 ,

Outh2.0 option is available in REST Receiver  Adapter,

also Outh2.0 option is available in REST Adapter Sender with Polling,

but we dont have Outh2.0 option in REST Sender Adapter in PI/PO System，

if I want to release the interface in this way ，please tell me how to solve this problem，thank you.

entry.title: SAP XI/PI excel to custom RFC
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

in my old custom RFC there are 3 import parameters ( IV_NAME, IV_AGE, IV_ADDRESS )

Existing

FieldIV_NAMEIV_AgeIV_Address
ValueNAME118Canada

need to add

FieldIV_CITY
ValueCITY1

in the XI/PO mapping the parameter is ( IV_NAME, IV_AGE, IV_ADDRESS )

In the new requirement we need to add and get the City from file created from XI/PO, I already adjusted the custom RFC and added the IV_CITY and the XI/PO team also added the new parameter IV_CITY to the mapping. while doing debugging, I checked the import parameter in the RFC and saw that the RFC did not receive the City value, what could be the cause of the issue?

entry.title: Disabling SSH-RSA algorithms to be compliant with SWIFT standards in SAP PO
entry.content:
Hello All,

I'm an SAP PO Consultant. We connect to numerous SFTP servers for picking and placing the files and we use SSH keys for Authentication purposes. We generate those keys and share the public key to them. But recently we're getting the requirements to disable the SSH-RSA algorithms to be compliant with their standards. We generate the keys and share the public key to the client to establish a connection.

So could anyone please guide me how to disable the weak ciphers/alogrithms? 

PS: We have tight deadline for this, so appreciate an early response 🙂

Regards,

Nikhil

entry.title: ECC user is getting locked due to multiple wrong login attempts
entry.content:
Hi,

We are facing an issue where ECC user is getting locked due to multiple wrong login attempts. We recently updated the password for the RFC user, and the same has been updated in all the RFC channels where we are using that username.

We also updated the same password in the NWA RFC destination. However, the user is still getting locked frequently.

Can anyone suggest all the places where we should look and update the new password for the ECC user?

entry.title: IDOC_ERROR_PARSE_FAILURE: Illegal value "" for attribute SEGMENT in <EDI_DC40 SEGMENT="1"> tag withi
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

we have recently migrated to PO from PI 7.4.

In a scenario of FILE to IDOC i am facing below error

""Error before sending due to IDoc parsing error: (7) IDOC_ERROR_PARSE_FAILURE: Illegal value "" for attribute SEGMENT in <EDI_DC40 SEGMENT="1"> tag within IDoc-XML for type <ZVESPRO>:
state=READING_ATTRIBUTE_VALUE, charPosition=98, lineNumber=1, columnNumber=99 ""

 

After my research , I found that SEGMENT field has to be mapped with Constant 1.

I have a question here , In 7.4 Idoc, Segment is mapped to constant "Null"(Blank parameter). and it working fine in production.

Now when coming to 7.5 , while we are trying to push the file , we are facing the error "IDOC_ERROR_PARSE_FAILURE: Illegal value "" for attribute SEGMENT".

IS it mandate to map segment to constant 1 in 7.5 PO ?

Can someone please help me on this. Would be very helpful.

entry.title: FCC not working properly failing with generating empty file - SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

There is a scenario where I have structure as below and FCC configured in sender channel mentioned -

<MT_XXX>

<Header> -- Few fields in the header section(details mentioned in sample file)

<Item> -- Few fields in the Item section(details mentioned in sample file)

<MT_XXX>

Error in the channel and even when I tried adding Key field in the structure following the blog mentioned below it is still failing by generating empty file (0 KB). and failing further in mapping stating premature end of file. 

End to End File Content Conversion (FCC) Using IFl... - SAP Community

PFA the csv file which is sample file.

Please suggest where this is going wrong and how can I try to resolve the same.

Thanks,

Nidhi Srivastava

entry.title: How do I edit the timestamp of the SFTP Adapter in SAP PO?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Is there a smart way to customize the SFTP adapter scenario where I can change the standard function Timestamp for the file name from out-kostenstelle-YYYYMMTT-HHmmss.xml to out-kostenstelle-YYYYMMTT.xml? 

We are using PI 7.5 single stack. 

Kind regards,

Miguel

entry.title: What are the ways to connect SAP PO to SQL DB ?
entry.content:
Hi,

The requirement is to send data from SAP ECC to SQL DB. We have the option of connecting to SQL DB via JDBC adapter. But the client is insisting on using HTTPS connectivity. 

How can this be achieved? 

Regards,

LR

entry.title: Import existing BPM source code project NWDS
entry.content:
Hello experts!

I was asked to modify the following existing BPM Process:

All I have been given is a DCs.zip file and inside the zip an directory called _comp with the following file structure inside:

Questions:

  • Is this file structure all I need to modify the existing BPM Process?
  • In which perspective should I import this structure?

I've been reading several posts like this one and to import a BPM Process an SCA file is required (which is not what I have)

Any guidance? Thank you in advance.

entry.title: how to create two outbound cremas idoc if same vendor is extended to two company codes
entry.content:
Hi, 

we want to create idoc based on company code in CREMAS message type if vendor is extended to two company codes then two separate messages should be created. Thanks

#CREMAS #SPLIT CREMAS OUTBOUND
